CCR informed us that the pump suddenly stopped during running condition. After that, the motor did not start.
Upon reaching the site, we found that liquor leakage from the adjacent pump P-576 had spread over the LPBS and the motor of P-577.
We cleaned the LPBS, carried out blowing, and replaced the push button element. We also inspected the PLC system and found everything to be in proper condition. However, in PMCC room the DMPR module 95/96 contact was found to be faulty, so we replaced existing module with a spare module.
After replacement, the trial was completed successfully, and the pump was taken back into line.

The existing K6 auxiliary contactor (3NO & 1NC) was used, where the 56/57 NO contact was not in line; only 2NO & 1NC were in line. Therefore, a new auxiliary contactor with 2NO & 2NC was used, and the DMPR 95/96 was connected to the NC of the new auxiliary contactor. NOW IT IS IN SPARE MODULE OR STANDBY MODULE
